learning_outcomes.description: Understand the significance of insects in environmental, economic, and social contexts
Characterise (identify) an insect specimen to the Order and Sub-Order taxonomic level
Prepare insect specimens for the purposes of insect identification to a professional standard
Read and appraise entomological literature critically

assessments.due_date:
11/09/2026 4:00 pm

A component of this task is assessed live during the timeslot that you have selected to use the SEM.
assessments.task_description:
Insect structures under a scanning electron microscope SEM are cool! This task will give you the opportunity to evidence your proficiency in

(1) specimen preparation for SEM imaging,

(2) usage, calibration and photography with an SEM, and

(3) science communication in the context of social media communication.

You will undertake work in pairs to use the SEM but the assessment is individual. Additional information will be provided in the course.

This assessment task is completed in-person. This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT). Students are advised that the use of AI or MT technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Online submission by Turnitin only by the due date and time. No hard copy or assignment cover sheets required. Submission via email is not accepted.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
16/10/2026 12:00 pm

The practical exam will take place during the timetabled practical session of Week 11.
assessments.task_description:
You will complete a practical exam which gives you the opportunity to demonstrate your entomological proficiencies in technique and knowledge. This involves a series of tasks assigned for you to complete by the end of the allocated time. This will also include written responses to questions. You must work individually and independently for this task.

This assessment task is completed in-person. This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT). Students are advised that the use of AI or MT technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You will attend the practical examination in person at the scheduled timeslot. This will occur in one of our biology laboratories, and you will be invigilated by academic staff members. You will be required to complete written tasks, and specimen preparation tasks. You will submit your written responses and prepared specimens by the conclusion of the exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
Collecting insects provides valuable insight into their habitat, distribution, biology and behaviour and makes their identification easier and more meaningful. You will assemble an insect collection (presented using professional standards) that evidence your ability to classify diverse insects (to Order level). You will complete this task individually, and should complete your own collection and identification. This includes humanely killing insects. You may purchase your own pinning board, box, and supplies if you intend to keep your own insect collection, or you may borrow equipment from us (loan), but these must be returned to us by the end of the course.

This assessment task is completed in-person. This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT). Students are advised that the use of AI or MT technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You will submit your insect collection at the end of the final practical of the semester (Week 13).
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.topic:
Practical 1 (Laboratory)

Receive insect collection kit; contents and collection assignment explained; pinning techniques demonstrated; displays of example collections and common pinning mistakes; practice insects provided for students to learn proper pinning and labelling techniques.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Practical 1 (Laboratory) Receive insect collection kit; contents and collection assignment explained; pinning techniques demonstrated; displays of example collections and common pinning mistakes; practice insects provided for students to learn proper pinning and labelling techniques.

learning_activities.topic:
Practical 2 (Laboratory)

Practice using dichotomous keys and identify insect orders; view examples of various orders; use of AI


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Practical 2 (Laboratory) Practice using dichotomous keys and identify insect orders; view examples of various orders; use of AI

Visit to Queensland Museum entomological and zoological curation department.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Excursion 1 Visit to Queensland Museum entomological and zoological curation department.

learning_activities.topic:
Practical 3 (Laboratory)

Continuing examples of insect orders and practise identification; explanation of scanning electron microscopy assignment; SEM techniques; project planning, specimen selection and preparation; time slot sign-up


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Practical 3 (Laboratory) Continuing examples of insect orders and practise identification; explanation of scanning electron microscopy assignment; SEM techniques; project planning, specimen selection and preparation; time slot sign-up

learning_activities.topic:
Scanning Electron Microscopy Week

Attend registered timeslot to use SEM and photograph specimens for assessment.


Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Scanning Electron Microscopy Week Attend registered timeslot to use SEM and photograph specimens for assessment.

learning_activities.topic:
Practical 4 (Fieldwork)

Walk around campus looking for insects; collecting methods demonstrated: sweep netting, beat sheeting; trapping methods explained: set up pitfall traps, Malaise traps, flight intercept traps. Any hand collected insects can be brought back to the lab for identification.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Practical 4 (Fieldwork) Walk around campus looking for insects; collecting methods demonstrated: sweep netting, beat sheeting; trapping methods explained: set up pitfall traps, Malaise traps, flight intercept traps. Any hand collected insects can be brought back to the lab for identification.

learning_activities.topic:
Practical 5 (Fieldwork)

Walk around campus looking for insects; collecting methods as before (sweep netting, beat sheeting); traps set up in previous weeks are cleared. Insects can be brought back to the lab for identification.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Practical 5 (Fieldwork) Walk around campus looking for insects; collecting methods as before (sweep netting, beat sheeting); traps set up in previous weeks are cleared. Insects can be brought back to the lab for identification.

learning_activities.topic:
Practical 6 (Laboratory)

Open invitation for you to bring in up to five specimens from your insect collection (which should be in progress by this time) to get feedback on quality and composition of your specimen preparation and pinning technique.
learning_activities.topic_description: Practical 6 (Laboratory) Open invitation for you to bring in up to five specimens from your insect collection (which should be in progress by this time) to get feedback on quality and composition of your specimen preparation and pinning technique.

learning_activities.topic:
Practical 7 (Fieldwork)

There will not be a practical session in the day during the usual timetabled session. Instead, you are invited to attend a night catch that will take place at the UQ Lakes Natural Amphitheatre to observe and collect nocturnal insects. We will include pizzas for everyone who attends!
